# UC-10.12.41 · NIST 800-53 Control Validation

## Description

Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families via automated checks against ingested security telemetry.

## Value

Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families via automated checks against ingested security telemetry.

## Implementation

Map each control to correlation searches; feed failures into GRC; annual assessor exports.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ES, compliance frameworks.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged `control_id`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map each control to correlation searches; feed failures into GRC; annual assessor exports.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=nist_800_53
| lookup nist_controls control_id OUTPUT family automated
| where automated="true" AND result="fail"
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail by control_id, host
| sort control_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIST 800-53 Control Validation** — Operationalizes AU, AC, SI, and IR families via automated checks against ingested security telemetry.

Documented **Data sources**: Mixed — auth, change, vuln indexes tagged `control_id`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ES, compliance frameworks. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where automated="true" AND result="fail"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by control_id, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix (control × system), Trend (fail rate), Table (open findings).

## SPL

```spl
index=security tag=nist_800_53
| lookup nist_controls control_id OUTPUT family automated
| where automated="true" AND result="fail"
| stats latest(_time) as last_fail by control_id, host
| sort control_id
```

## Visualization

Matrix (control × system), Trend (fail rate), Table (open findings).
